prompt:
In the SMEFT, which of the following symmetries are required to be obeyed by every operator?

1. Lorentz Symmetry
2. Poincare symmetry
3. CP symmetry
4. CPT symmetry

Choices:
A. 1,2
B. 1,3,4
C. 1,2,4
D. 3,4

The gastric mucosa includes multiple kinds of cells. Which statement about these cells is accurate?

Choices:
A. Parietal cells secrete pepsinogen, the inactive precursor of pepsin
B. G cells secrete the enzyme amylase
C. Chief cells the hormone gastrin (which stimulates acid secretion).
D. Mucous cells secrete the hormone secretin
E. Enterochromaffin-like cells secrete the enzyme lipase
F. Parietal cells secrete the hormone ghrelin
G. Mucous cells secrete the glycoprotein mucin
H. Chief cells secrete bile acid
I. D cells secrete the hormone cholecystokinin

A viral infectious disease has broken out in your city, and you are tasked with creating a molecular diagnostic kit for rapid detection of this retrovirus. Which strategy would you use to design the kit?

Choices:
A. First you identify the IgG antibodies for the infection. You then develop an ELISA kit targeting IgG antibodies for quick 
 and accurate diagnosis.
B. First you identify the virus by performing cDNA sequencing.  You then develop a real time PCR kit for quick and accurate diagnosis.
C. First you identify the virus using the information about the symptoms, provided by the affected patients. You then design a nested PCR kit based on the information for quick diagnosis.
D. First you identify the virus by performing DNA sequencing. You then develop a PCR kit for  quick and accurate diagnosis

Suppose a system has Hamiltonian operator \(H=\varepsilon\,\vec{\sigma}\cdot\vec{n}\), where \(\vec{n}\) is an arbitrary unit vector, \(\varepsilon\) is a constant with dimensions of energy, and the components of \(\vec{\sigma}\) are the Pauli spin matrices. Determine the eigenvalues of this Hamiltonian operator.

Choices:
A. +\varepsilon \hbar/2, - \varepsilon \hbar/2
B. + \varepsilon, -\varepsilon
C. +1, -1
D. +\hbar/2, -\hbar/2

In recent years, a state has experienced a major increase in its population of retired people. State health officials are worried that many senior citizens may be subjected to price gouging involving certain medical supplies and services. To address these fraudulent sales practices, the state legislature passed a statute barring non-physicians from selling hearing aids. The statute also states that non-physician sellers who are already in the business of selling hearing aids are not affected. After the law takes effect, a non-physician relocates to the state and seeks to start a hearing-aid sales business. Upon being told that the statute forbids him from doing so, he files suit challenging the law’s constitutionality. The most probable outcome is that the state law will be declared

Choices:
A. unconstitutional, because it violates the commerce clause, since Congress has plenary power to regulate any activity that has any appreciable effect on interstate commerce.
B. unconstitutional, because it creates an unfair trade barrier for non-physicians wanting to start a business in the state.
C. constitutional, as it aims to protect a vulnerable segment of the population from potential exploitation.
D. constitutional, because there is a rational basis for distinguishing between non-physicians are not so engaged.
E. unconstitutional, because it discriminates between non-physicians already in the business and those wanting to enter it.
F. unconstitutional, because it restricts the rights of non-physicians to conduct a legitimate business.
G. constitutional, because it falls under the state's power to regulate healthcare and related services.
H. constitutional, because it does not infringe upon the rights of non-physicians not already in the business of selling hearing aids.
I. constitutional, because a state has the power to regulate any phase of local business, even though such regulations may have some effect on interstate commerce, provided that Congress has not enacted legislation regarding the subject matter.

Under acidic conditions, the Pinacol-Pinacolone rearrangement converts a pinacol, which has two neighboring alcohol groups, into a pinacolone. The mechanism involves protonation of one alcohol group, then a 1,2-hydride shift, producing a ketone and a rearranged molecular framework.

Determine the unknown compound A and the product B in these Pinacol-Pinacolone rearrangements:

A + H2SO4 ---> 2,2-di-p-tolylcyclohexan-1-one  
methyl 2,3-dihydroxy-2-(p-tolyl)butanoate + H2SO4 ---> B

Choices:
A. A = 1-(hydroxydi-p-tolylmethyl)cyclopentan-1-ol, B = methyl 3-oxo-2-(p-tolyl)butanoate
B. A = 1-(hydroxydi-p-tolylmethyl)cyclohexan-1-ol, B = methyl 3-oxo-2-(p-tolyl)butanoate
C. A = 1-(hydroxydi-p-tolylmethyl)cyclopentan-1-ol, B = methyl 2-methyl-3-oxo-2-(p-tolyl)propanoate
D. A = 1-(hydroxydi-p-tolylmethyl)cyclohexan-1-ol, B = methyl 2-methyl-3-oxo-2-(p-tolyl)propanoate

Suppose a vector field f(r) is directed purely along the radial direction in spherical coordinates and its magnitude decreases from the origin according to f(r) = 1/r^2. Calculate the volume integral of its divergence, ∇.f, over the interior of a sphere of radius R centred at the origin. Here, both ∇ and f are to be understood as vectors. Which of the following is the most appropriate value for this integral?

Choices:
A. 4/3 π R
B. 4 π
C. 0
D. 1

Mr. James falls in the 50% income tax bracket. He has the option of purchasing, at par value, corporate bonds paying 8% interest or municipal bonds paying 4(1/2)% interest. Which investment would be wiser?

Choices:
A. both municipal and corporate bonds
B. municipal bonds
C. neither municipal nor corporate bonds
D. investing in stocks instead
E. corporate bonds with a different interest rate
F. municipal bonds after considering potential changes in tax-exempt status
G. real estate investment trusts as an alternative investment
H. corporate bonds
I. corporate bonds after considering a potential tax reform
J. municipal bonds with an additional state tax benefit

Given that the vapor pressure of CCl_4 (carbon tetrachloride) is .132 atm at 23°C and H^1 = 32,000 (J / mole), calculate its vapor pressure at 38°C.

Choices:
A. .275 atm
B. .250 atm
C. .225 atm
D. .300 atm
E. .200 atm
F. .400 atm
G. .150 atm
H. .132 atm
I. .175 atm
J. .325 atm

What effect would a rising national debt have on the market for U.S. dollars and on the dollar’s value relative to other currencies? MARKET FOR THE DOLLAR     VALUE OF THE DOLLAR

Choices:
A. Increased supply     Depreciating
B. Decreased supply     Appreciating
C. No change in supply     Appreciating
D. Increased demand     Depreciating
E. Decreased demand     Appreciating
F. Increased demand     Appreciating
G. Decreased supply     Depreciating
H. Increased supply     Appreciating
I. No change in demand     Depreciating

What effect would a rising national debt have on the market for U.S. dollars and on the dollar’s value relative to other currencies? MARKET FOR THE DOLLAR     VALUE OF THE DOLLAR

Choices:
A. Increased supply     Depreciating
B. Decreased supply     Appreciating
C. No change in supply     Appreciating
D. Increased demand     Depreciating
E. Decreased demand     Appreciating
F. Increased demand     Appreciating
G. Decreased supply     Depreciating
H. Increased supply     Appreciating
I. No change in demand     Depreciating
